genValidProfiles.name: pass-oi-default-scalar
genValidProfiles.intent: Seed-indexed OI-D scalar trigger-smoke modules covering direct scalar identities plus local-carried and local.tee/commuted add-zero variants; future work should replace the deterministic three-case selector with broader randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and existing-producer wrappers.

genValidProfiles.name: pass-oi-local-facts
genValidProfiles.intent: Seed-indexed OI-E local-fact trigger-smoke modules covering direct local.set/local.get sign-extension facts, local.tee carried add-zero, local-carried mask use, no-param helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, effectful helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, trapping helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, local.tee helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, wide local-carried mask facts, local-carried helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, effectful local-carried helper-call existing-producer sign-extension facts, i32.wrap_i64 / i64.extend_i32_u wrap/extend mask facts, shifted-mask facts from local-carried i32.shr_u / i64.shr_u producers, wide shifted-mask facts with wider shifted i32/i64 carriers, and typed select-produced i32/i64 mask facts; future work should add randomized maxBits/signExt/nullness fact constructors, broader non-i32 carriers, broader masks, broader select/control producers, and broader source-backed effect/trap wrappers.

genValidProfiles.implementation: src/validate/gen_valid.mbt special-cases this profile to emit one of fourteen validating local-fact trigger-smoke modules selected by seed: direct sign-extension facts, local.tee add-zero facts, local mask facts, a helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module, an effectful helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module with private-global writes, a trapping helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module with i32/i64 divide-by-zero helpers, a local.tee helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module, a wide local-carried mask module, a local-carried helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module, an effectful local-carried helper-call existing-producer sign-extension module with private-global writes, a wrap/extend mask module using local-carried i32.wrap_i64 and i64.extend_i32_u producers, a shifted-mask module using local-carried i32.shr_u and i64.shr_u producers, a wide-shifted-mask module using wider local-carried i32.shr_u and i64.shr_u producers, or a select-produced-mask module using typed i32/i64 select producers before all-ones masks. src/validate/gen_valid_tests.mbt asserts baseline fact coverage, stable labels, validation, and distinct seed-indexed trigger signatures; src/fuzz/main_wbtest.mbt asserts profile_case_label metadata includes shifted-mask, wide-shifted-mask, and select-produced-mask cases.

genValidProfiles.name: pass-oi-boolean-select
genValidProfiles.intent: Seed-indexed OI-F boolean/select trigger-smoke modules covering direct if/select shells, source-backed effectful identical-select conditions, dead trapping if arms, and value-if/drop shells; branch-hint/code-metadata and never-fold/no-reorder option surfaces remain blocked until Starshine has representation/pass-option support.
genValidProfiles.ordinaryCommand: bun scripts/pass-fuzz-compare.ts --count 10000 --seed 0x5eed --pass optimize-instructions --gen-valid-profile pass-oi-boolean-select --out-dir .tmp/pass-fuzz-oi-boolean-select --jobs auto --starshine-bin target/native/release/build/cmd/cmd.exe
genValidProfiles.implementation: src/validate/gen_valid.mbt special-cases this profile to emit one of four validating trigger-smoke modules selected by seed: direct constant if/select shells, an effectful identical-select condition through a private-global helper call, a constant-live if with a dead trapping divide-by-zero arm, or a value-if/drop shell. src/validate/gen_valid_tests.mbt asserts stable labels, validation, select/call/global/trap signatures, and distinct seed-indexed cases.

rows.notes: pass-oi-default-scalar now emits three deterministic seed-indexed trigger-smoke cases for direct, local-carried, and local.tee/commuted OI-D shapes instead of one fixed module. The count-3 grouped OI-D sweep reaches comparison and reports three raw mismatches with no validation/generator/property/command failures; classify them as open scalar parity evidence until implemented or measured, not automatic semantic-safe drift. Future work should replace the seed-indexed smoke selector with randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and wrappers around existing producers. The sweep runner prints result summaries (and can load them with --summarize-existing), so future OI-D/OI-E triage should start from profile-case and case-label status groups before opening individual WAT diffs. A 2026-06-27 representative diff pass classified the three trigger labels in classifiedCaseLabels. The narrow literal double-eqz final-folding candidate is now implemented and covered. The fresh count-3 OI-D sweep shows residual mismatches only from Starshine extra folds of constant compare and/or constant sign-extension shapes; those sampled residuals are measured smaller Starshine outputs (-5/-2/-2 canonical wasm bytes) and all outputs validate, but the row remains open until broader randomized trigger coverage and/or source-backed family policy decides whether to retain or align these output-shape wins.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-default-scalar:local-tee-commuted-add-zero
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant compare and constant sign-extension folds
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps one pure constant i32.eq and explicit constant sign-extension opcodes; Starshine folds both constant i32.eq forms and direct constant sign extensions to constants. After the literal double-eqz fix both sides agree on i32.const 1. The sampled Starshine canonical wasm is 91 bytes vs Binaryen 96 bytes (-5 bytes), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 fresh OI-D count-3 sweep at .tmp/oi-parity-sweep-oi-d-double-eqz-fix-smoke; stat measured canonical wasm byte deltas; wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine canonical outputs.

classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.

rows.releaseImpact: P0: release decision required; default scalar identities are common enough that remaining known scalar drift must either be implemented or explicitly accepted before OI closeout.
rows.remainingCloseoutWork: Collapse the accumulated OI-D residuals into a finite scalar table: known implemented identities, measured sampled Starshine-win residuals, and true missing Binaryen scalar rewrites. Do not add generator labels; use existing pass-oi-default-scalar and OI-D grouped evidence first.
rows.nextCloseoutAction: Classify the existing OI-D default-scalar residual family and implement any small confirmed parity gap with red-first tests; otherwise propose a v0.1 accepted-release-boundary with exact reopen criteria and a finite signoff command.
rows.closeoutRationale: Existing docs show many scalar subsets implemented and some sampled smaller Starshine residuals, but the row still says broad default-scalar breadth is underclassified. That is not closeout-ready and should be treated as implementation/finite-classification work, not more smoke breadth.
rows.reopenCriteria: Any true semantic mismatch, validation failure, size-losing unimplemented scalar rewrite without accepted rationale, new Binaryen lit/source scalar family not mapped in the matrix, or a failed finite OI-D signoff lane.

rows.title: LocalScanner-style maxBits/signExtBits/unsigned range facts
rows.binaryenOracleMode: compare-pass plus focused local-carried oracle fixtures when generator support lands
rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The latest 2026-07-01 OI-E profile broadening adds a source-visible select-produced-mask case using typed i32/i64 select producers feeding all-ones masks. Red-first src/validate/gen_valid_tests.mbt::OI-D and OI-E trigger-smoke profiles select distinct seed-indexed cases failed at seed 0x5efa because pass-oi-local-facts still selected oi-local-facts:effectful-existing-producer-sign-extension, then passed after src/validate/gen_valid.mbt widened pass-oi-local-facts to fourteen cases. Direct .tmp/oi-e-select-mask-direct140-20260701 compared 140/140 with 62 normalized matches, 78 raw mismatches, and zero validation/generator/property/command failures; grouped .tmp/oi-e-select-mask-count140-20260701 compared 140/140 with 45 normalized matches, 95 raw mismatches, and zero failures. Manual wasm-tools validate --features all accepted all 312 direct and grouped residual raw/canonical artifacts. The new select-produced residual bucket is sampled Starshine-win evidence: direct residuals n=13 preserve i32.and 13/13, i64.and 13/13, local.set 52/52, local.get 52/52, and drop 52/52 while Starshine removes redundant sign-extension shells i32.extend8_s 13/0 and i64.extend32_s 13/0, with raw/canonical/WAT deltas -26/-26/-637; grouped residuals n=13 preserve i32.and 21/21, i64.and 13/13, local.set 57/57, local.get 61/61, and drop 89/89 while Starshine removes sign-extension and pure-equality shells, with deltas -20/-38/-781. The whole grouped residual split is aggregate Starshine-smaller at -319/-417/-6810 raw/canonical/WAT. OI-E remains open for randomized maxBits/signExt/nullness facts, broader non-i32 carriers, broader masks, broader select/control producers, and broader source-backed effect/trap wrappers. This remains non-descriptor evidence and does not advance OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT.

rows.notes: Latest 2026-07-01 select-produced-mask evidence: the generator test failed at seed 0x5efa until src/validate/gen_valid.mbt emitted typed i32/i64 select producers feeding all-ones masks. The direct count140 lane compared 140/140 with 62 normalized matches, 78 raw mismatches, and zero failures; the grouped count140 lane compared 140/140 with 45 normalized matches, 95 raw mismatches, and zero failures; all 312 direct/grouped residual raw/canonical artifacts validate. The select-produced residuals are sampled Starshine-win output drift, not broad LocalScanner closure: the direct bucket preserves i32.and 13/13, i64.and 13/13, local.set 52/52, local.get 52/52, and drop 52/52 while Starshine removes redundant i32.extend8_s 13/0 and i64.extend32_s 13/0 for raw/canonical/WAT deltas -26/-26/-637; the grouped bucket preserves i32.and 21/21, i64.and 13/13, local.set 57/57, local.get 61/61, and drop 89/89 while Starshine also removes pure equality shells for deltas -20/-38/-781. OI-E remains open for randomized LocalScanner maxBits/signExt/nullness facts, broader non-i32 carriers, broader masks, local.tee/local-carried wrappers around broader existing producers, broader select/control producers, and source-backed effect/trap boundaries. Do not use any of this non-descriptor evidence for OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT.

rows.releaseImpact: P0: LocalScanner facts affect common integer/mask/sign-extension cleanup. v0.1 can only close this row after either implementing broader fact parity or explicitly accepting a narrow release boundary.
rows.remainingCloseoutWork: Stop adding one-off local-fact labels. Decide the finite remaining set: randomized maxBits/signExt/nullness, non-i32 carriers, broader masks, broader select/control producers, and effect/trap wrappers. Existing sampled residuals are mostly Starshine-win after validation/opcode/size review, but that does not close broad LocalScanner parity.
rows.nextCloseoutAction: Use the accumulated OI-E evidence to write a finite LocalScanner closeout table: covered sampled shapes, known implementation gaps, and proposed v0.1 release boundary/reopen criteria. Only implement if a current residual is proven size-losing or semantically risky.
rows.closeoutRationale: 2026-06-30/2026-07-01 OI-E lanes validated hundreds of residual artifacts and measured smaller Starshine output for many sampled masks/producers, but the row still explicitly lacks broad randomized LocalScanner behavior. Therefore the state remains mismatch-needs-implementation rather than covered.
rows.reopenCriteria: Any sampled OI-E bucket with raw/canonical size loss, missing validation, unpreserved effects/traps/locals/masks, broad LocalScanner source/lit behavior not represented by Starshine, or final signoff mismatch not classified as a measured Starshine win.

rows.title: Memory/load/store and bulk-memory tiny-lowering boundaries
rows.binaryenOracleMode: compare-pass normalized WAT with all-features; focused rows must keep trap-relaxed zero-size mode separate
rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The OI-G trigger profile has twenty-seven seed-indexed/BodyStream-randomized trigger-smoke-plus cases, adding source-visible shared-memory seed-derived aligned-address full-width atomic xchg restore coverage to the prior runtime store8-mask, full-width xchg, shared wait/notify, runtime restore/fill/call/localtee/select, memory64, multi-memory, and tiny bulk surfaces. The latest grouped count405 runtime run compares 405/405 with 333 normalized matches, 72 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, and runtime checked/unsupported/failed 185/220/0. The new shared-atomic-random-xchg label samples 11/11 residuals; inspected representatives preserve the same atomic load/xchg restore contract, runtime-supported samples are equal, and the residual is Binaryen choosing folded i32.const address spelling for one i64 atomic load where Starshine keeps offset=8 on the seed-derived base. Residual buckets are all Starshine smaller or canonical-equal except WAT text increases in atomic offset-folding buckets. OI-G remains open for broader grouped mismatch reduction, randomized atomic RMW/cmpxchg beyond xchg, true shared-memory ordering/concurrency, broader wait/notify including blocking behavior, broader source-visible mutation/restore shapes, true live trap/effect ordering, broader store/address commutation, randomized memory64/multi-memory wrappers, raw/text residual policy, and exact cross-memory/runtime execution where needed. OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT remains blocked until true descriptor-compatible evidence exists; this non-descriptor OI-G evidence must not close it.

implementationNotes: 2026-06-27: oi-memory-size-boundary is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over dead memory.copy/memory.fill tiny-size variants for i32 memories; row remains mismatch pending live boundary, memory64, trigger-biased generator, and executed sweep evidence.

2026-06-27: oi-commuted-operands is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over synthetic i32 constant add/and/eq operand-order pairs; row remains mismatch pending memory-address/store-mask trigger wrappers and live memory boundary variants.

2026-06-27: oi-effectful-sibling is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over a private mutable i32 global and stack-neutral effectful shells; row remains mismatch pending live memory/bulk wrappers, memory64, dynamic operands, and trapping sibling variants.

2026-06-28: red-first dead-if/raw-skip coverage now lowers size 1/2/4/8/16 memory.copy/fill in dead synthetic shells while preserving zero-size and size-3/5 bulk spellings; the sampled memory-size-boundary raw residual is now classified as structural/code-section drift from extra load/store/atomic offset folding plus one fewer local, while OI-G remains open on broader memory64/dynamic/live effect/trap surfaces and raw-byte signoff alignment if required.

2026-06-28: pass-oi-memory-bulk is now a seed-indexed trigger-smoke profile with direct i32 tiny-bulk/load-store, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk, and live private-global plus dead-trap boundary cases. The direct count-3 lane normalized-matches all three profile cases; the grouped count-5 transform lane leaves three classified output-shape/cross-family residuals and keeps OI-G open for randomized memory wrappers, live ordering, atomics/store masks, and broader memory64/dynamic evidence.

2026-06-28: pass-oi-memory-bulk expanded from three to six trigger-smoke-plus cases with existing-producer/no-param-call wrapped bulk, store-mask, and dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands. Direct count-18 samples all six labels; grouped count-6 exposes a size-losing store-mask + dead memory-size-boundary residual and a count>=11 generator/metamorphic command failure when memory-size-boundary reaches a later memory64 case, so OI-G remains open.

2026-06-28: red-first raw partial coverage now drops redundant i32/i64 low-bit masks before narrow stores after a dead memory-size-boundary-like shell. The grouped store-mask residual is no longer the prior size-losing canonical parity gap; it is reduced to the same low-byte-store Starshine-win candidate as the direct lane, with a remaining raw-size residual and OI-G still open.

2026-06-28: red-first raw partial coverage now rewrites adjacent nonnegative signed div/rem-by-zero trap spellings to Binaryen's unsigned forms in raw-skipped optimize-instructions functions. This removes the sampled dead scalar trap spelling residual from the grouped OI-G live-effect and multi-memory labels; it is a spelling/parity reduction, not live OI-G trap-order closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-zero-memory-boundary, a reachable zero-size memory.copy/fill smoke transform for memory32 and memory64-only modules. This broadens OI-G grouped evidence toward live no-op bulk-memory boundaries but is not live trap-order or atomics closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-zero-memory-boundary, a reachable zero-size memory.copy/fill smoke transform for memory32 and memory64-only modules. Grouped count-70 samples 11 live-zero cases and all 11 match; this broadens live no-op bulk-memory evidence but is not live trap-order, atomics, or randomized memory closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-copy-boundary, a guarded reachable length-1 memory.copy-from-self plus byte load/drop smoke transform. This broadens OI-G beyond zero-size live no-ops into live nonzero copy/read evidence, but still does not cover memory.fill restore wrappers, atomics, randomized addresses, or true live trap ordering.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-copy-boundary, a guarded reachable length-1 memory.copy-from-self plus byte load/drop smoke transform. Its first grouped run exposed a raw-skip i32.ne(memory.size, 0) if-condition spelling residual; a red-first raw partial fix now matches Binaryen's memory.size condition spelling. The post-fix grouped count-77 leaves only direct-tiny offset-folding/raw residuals under this transform, with zero canonical size-losing failure dirs.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-end-fill-restore-boundary, a guarded live nonzero end-byte fill/read transform that computes memory.size * 65536 - 1 and restores that byte before load/drop. This broadens nonzero-address evidence but is not randomized-address, atomics, true live trap-order, or OI-G closure.

2026-06-29: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-atomic-wait-notify-boundary, a private shared-memory nonblocking wait/notify transform. It appends a private shared i32 memory with min=max=1, guards memory.size != 0, emits memory.atomic.notify, memory.atomic.wait32, and memory.atomic.wait64, and chooses wait expected values that differ from zero-initialized private memory with timeout 0 so the waits do not block and source-visible memories are not mutated. Grouped count350 at .tmp/oi-g-atomic-wait-notify-count350-20260629 compared 182/350 transform-applicable cases with 160 normalized matches, 22 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, and the new transform 9/9 matches. Manual failure-dir review found zero validation failures and zero raw/canonical/normalized-WAT Starshine-larger dirs. This broadens wait/notify evidence only; it does not close true shared-memory ordering, source-visible shared-memory effects, blocking wait behavior, full-width/source-visible xchg, OI-G, or OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT.

rows.releaseImpact: P0: memory/load-store is release-relevant, but current residuals are mostly sampled Starshine-win output drift with runtime equality where executable; exact shared ordering/concurrency and memory64+multi-memory runtime execution remain finite release boundaries.
rows.remainingCloseoutWork: Do not broaden labels. Convert the existing OI-G runtime/validation/opcode evidence into a release boundary: covered tiny bulk/store-mask/restore samples, unsupported Node memory64+multi-memory runtime lanes, and out-of-scope true shared-memory concurrency/blocking wait behavior.
rows.nextCloseoutAction: Produce an OI-G closeout-boundary docs slice that either accepts the current memory64/shared runtime limitations for v0.1 or identifies a single concrete implementation gap; cite runtime checked/unsupported/failed counts and opcode/size deltas.
rows.closeoutRationale: Recent grouped/runtime lanes report zero runtime semantic mismatches where executable and manual validation of residual artifacts, while unsupported cases are tool/runtime limits or true concurrency semantics not exercised by the Node lane. This can be a v0.1 boundary candidate but is not family-covered.
rows.reopenCriteria: Runtime semantic mismatch, validation failure, size-losing memory residual, unpreserved memory operation/order/effect/trap in inspected WAT/opcodes, Node/tool support for memory64+multi-memory changing the executable set, or product decision to require true shared wait/notify/concurrency parity.

rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The profile-backed OI-H grouped smoke has source-backed pass-oi-call-ref trigger labels for local-carried, block-produced, void return_call_ref, direct ref.func, typed table.get call_ref, non-void typed table.get return_call_ref, i32 argument-bearing direct ref.func call_ref, i32 argument-bearing local-carried ref.func call_ref, i32/i64 argument plus i64-result multivalue ref.func call_ref, and i32 select-produced argument ref.func call_ref targets. The latest 2026-06-30 argument-select slice widens the profile to ten cases. Targeted direct seed 0x5ef6 matches the new argument-select label 1/1; grouped .tmp/oi-h-argument-select-count40-20260630 compares 40/40 with 18 normalized matches and 22 residuals, with argument-select-target matching 1/1. Manual validation accepted all 44 grouped residual raw/canonical artifacts. The residual split has aggregate Starshine deltas -42/-299/-2006 raw/canonical/WAT and contains familiar dropped-ref.func/direct-call output drift buckets; no residual is the new select-argument label. Broader argument localization through tables/complex locals, live table-state known-target directization, broader return_call_ref, broader multivalue target/argument variants, and effect/trap target-boundary sweeps remain open.

rows.notes: oi-call-ref-target-wrapper is implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner: it appends a private no-op target and prepends direct ref.func/call_ref, block-returned ref.func/call_ref, typed-select target/call_ref, and dead call_ref shells to existing defined functions. A default GenValid one-case compare produced one raw mismatch with no validation/generator/command failures; the earlier pass-oi-call-ref seed-0x5eed validity blocker from stringref/exn ref pools is fixed and the profile-backed smoke now reaches comparison. The profile-backed representative first exposed a large size-losing OI-H parity gap where Binaryen directized synthetic ref.func call_ref wrappers while Starshine left call_ref/ref.func spelling. The raw skip path now directizes adjacent ref.func/block-ref.func, constant-select ref.func, and single-use local.set(ref.func)/local.get call_ref/return_call_ref wrappers, and it trims guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast trap tails before returning a skipped function, without widening the full HOT raw gate. The latest one-case target-wrapper smoke still mismatches, but OI-H call target spelling is no longer the sampled residual: Starshine and Binaryen both have 0 call_ref/table.get residuals, 20 direct `call $5` occurrences, 0 ref.test/ref.cast/br_on_cast/br_on_cast_fail, and five `unreachable` roots. The remaining 18-byte raw size gap is local-section/ref.func-drop output shape drift: Starshine drops the inserted `ref.func` uses while Binaryen keeps five dropped `ref.func` instructions. A follow-up count-3 grouped OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` mixed the target-wrapper, local-carried, and effectful-sibling transforms; all three cases still mismatched, but every sampled Starshine and Binaryen output had call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, and table.get=0. The target-wrapper case remains the local-section/ref.func-drop shape drift; the local-carried case is local-section/dropped-null shape drift after successful call_ref cleanup; the effectful-sibling case is cross-family effectful private-global if/scalar spelling drift after successful call_ref cleanup. A 2026-06-30 measurement validates all grouped raw/canonical artifacts and shows the residual set is raw-size-losing overall (Starshine raw +129 bytes) while canonical and WAT are smaller (-72/-3473); keep the residual classified as output-shape/cross-family drift, not broad OI-H closure. Future OI-H work should run broader table/local/return_call_ref/multivalue sweeps to find true remaining call_ref directization gaps instead of over-mining these cross-family residuals. A timeout-conscious count6 refresh at `.tmp/oi-h-focused-count6-20260630` avoided the earlier larger-run timeout and sampled two cases for each forwarded transform. It did not expose new call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get residuals: all sampled outputs stayed target-clean on both sides. The residual remains raw-size-losing in aggregate while canonical and WAT are smaller, so OI-H stays open for broader target-generation rather than another fix to the already-clean sampled wrappers. A later bounded count8 attempt timed out at 300s before summary emission, so current OI-H refreshes should stay very small or get explicit timeout planning. The completed count4 follow-up at `.tmp/oi-h-bounded-count4-20260630` reconfirms the same classification on a fresh bounded lane: all 16 residual raw/canonical artifacts validate, all sampled outputs remain target-clean for call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get/call_indirect/return_call_indirect on both sides, and residuals are still raw-size-losing output-shape/cross-family drift rather than a live target directization gap. The return-table target-wrapper representative from `.tmp/oi-h-return-table-target-count24-20260630` was inspected separately: the raw +2 byte representative difference comes from Binaryen retaining a dropped ref.func shell that Starshine removes, while Starshine wins canonical/WAT and both outputs preserve the lowered return_call_indirect target. Do not reintroduce that debris just to align raw bytes without stronger raw-size evidence. The eight-case pass-oi-call-ref profile now includes i32 argument-bearing direct and local-carried ref.func call_ref targets. Direct profile smoke matched each new argument label 1/1; grouped residuals under target-wrapper transforms still directize the argument calls and differ only in dropped-ref.func wrapper debris, so this is sampled argument-bearing evidence rather than broad argument-localization, multivalue, table-state, or descriptor closure. A follow-up full residual split of `.tmp/oi-h-argument-local-target-count32-20260630` classifies all latest grouped buckets rather than only the new argument-local samples: the lane has zero validation failures, no residual Starshine call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get, and every residual is either sampled Starshine direct-call cleanup or output-shape/cross-family dropped-ref.func drift. The split is evidence for the current grouped lane only and does not close broad OI-H argument localization, multivalue, table-state, or OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT. The ten-case pass-oi-call-ref profile now also includes an i32 select-produced argument target. Targeted direct seed 0x5ef6 and the grouped count40 lane matched argument-select-target 1/1, while residuals stayed in pre-existing dropped-ref.func/direct-call output-drift buckets; keep OI-H open for broader argument localization/table-state/return_call_ref/multivalue/effect-trap sweeps and do not use this as OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT evidence.

rows.releaseImpact: P1: direct call_ref/table.get/argument cases have sampled evidence, but broader table-state proof, return_call_ref, multivalue and effect/trap target sweeps remain non-P0 for v0.1 unless product scope changes.
rows.remainingCloseoutWork: Condense existing target-clean and argument evidence into covered subsets versus explicit deferred target-state/localization work. Avoid more profile labels unless a known table-state or return_call_ref implementation lands.
rows.nextCloseoutAction: Either implement a single known target-state directization gap red-first or mark OI-H as v0.1 defer-with-reopen-criteria using existing validation/opcode/size evidence.
rows.closeoutRationale: Sampled outputs are often target-clean and canonical/WAT smaller, but some residuals are raw-size-losing output-shape drift and broad call_ref directization is not complete. This is finite defer/implementation work, not P0 closeout.
rows.reopenCriteria: Residual Starshine call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get in a supported known-target case, raw/canonical size loss without accepted rationale, validation failure, or new source-backed call_ref target shape promoted into v0.1 scope.

rows.notes: OI-M has extensive one-off boundary research through many effect-count rows. The family workflow now provides forwarded selected-lane, effectful-sibling, dead trap-shaped sibling, and local-carried smoke transforms, and now has trigger-biased tuple-profile constructors including helper-call-produced selected-lane, local.tee-produced selected-lane, select-produced selected-lane, selected trapping lane, selected effectful lane, first live effectful non-selected sibling smoke, first live trapping non-selected sibling smoke, and first effectful select-operand selected-lane smoke, first trapping select-operand selected-lane smoke, and first multi-selected effectful i64 lane smoke, but still needs broader existing-producer/effect/trap selected-lane wrappers beyond the current smoke cases, broader tuple-scratch localization/reconstruction constructors beyond the current scratch-localized and effectful scratch-localized selected-lane smokes, runtime semantic execution where relevant, and randomized multivalue breadth to stop producing one boundary doc per lane/effect count. The first synthetic `oi-tuple-selected-lane` smoke mismatch is classified in `classifiedCaseLabels`: for pure constant multivalue block lane extraction, Starshine keeps nested block-expression localization while Binaryen scalarizes to more straight-line local sets; the sampled Starshine canonical/raw wasm is smaller and both outputs validate, so this is a sampled Starshine-win candidate, not a closure of the broader tuple-scratch reconstruction family. The 2026-06-30 count102 scratch-localized residual split classified all 102 grouped residuals: every label was raw/canonical smaller for Starshine and WAT-larger from retained block-result spelling, with aggregate deltas -6262/-2831/+57594. The 2026-07-01 count108 effectful-scratch refresh sampled all 18 labels, including four effectful-scratch-localized residuals whose helper call/global.set/local traffic is preserved while Starshine is raw/canonical smaller (-92/-20) and WAT-larger (+784); the full lane had aggregate deltas -2745/-713/+24520 and runtime equality for all nine exported runtime samples. This reduces grouped unknowns but remains sampled output-drift evidence rather than tuple-scratch reconstruction/localization closure.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: pass-oi-tuple:oi-tuple-selected-lane-smoke
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: sampled size-winning representation drift over pure synthetic multivalue selected-lane smoke; broader OI-M tuple-scratch localization remains open
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform prepends pure constant multivalue block selected-lane shells. Binaryen scalarizes the shell into many straight-line local.set/local.tee operations; Starshine keeps nested block-result expressions around the selected lane. Starshine canonical wasm is 4100 bytes vs Binaryen 4326 bytes (-226), raw wasm is 3975 vs 4229 bytes (-254), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Do not treat this smoke as OI-M signoff. Upgrade pass-oi-tuple / oi-tuple-selected-lane to wrap existing tuple producers with effectful/trapping siblings and selected-lane variants; preserve this sample as a Starshine-win candidate unless broader probes show semantic or size regressions.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 diff/stat/wasm-tools validate of .tmp/oi-tuple-selected-lane-compare-smoke/failures/case-000001-gen-valid-transform-oi-tuple-selected-lane.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-tuple:local-carried-selected-lane
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: sampled size-winning multivalue selected-lane representation drift over a local-carried selected value; not tuple-scratch family closure
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Both tools eliminate tuple.make/tuple.extract. Binaryen scalarizes the generated local-carried multivalue selected-lane shells to straight-line local.set/local.tee traffic, while Starshine keeps block-result expressions. Raw wasm Starshine/Binaryen is 221/321 bytes and canonical wasm is 321/374 bytes; both raw/canonical outputs validate. Normalized WAT is larger for Starshine because of retained block-result spelling.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as sampled Starshine-win candidate only. Reopen for validation failure, observable value/effect/trap ordering drift, Starshine-larger raw/canonical wasm, non-local-carried producer hazards, or Binaryen source evidence requiring scalarized tuple-scratch spelling.
